name: novelbits-logscope
description: Real-time embedded log viewer with Bluetooth LE HCI decoding — Zephyr logs with filtering, search, fault detection, and Wireshark export

viewsWelcome.contents:
Connect a device to start viewing real-time logs.

[$(plug) Connect Device](command:logscope.connect)

[$(open-preview) Open Log Viewer](command:logscope.open)

[$(book) Get Started Guide](command:logscope.openWalkthrough)

Supports J-Link RTT and Serial UART transports.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with LogScope
walkthroughs.description: Connect to your embedded device and start viewing real-time logs in 3 steps.

steps.title: Connect Your Device
steps.description:
LogScope supports **J-Link RTT** (debug probe) and **Serial UART** (USB serial).

Click below to pick your transport, select a device, and connect.

[Connect Device](command:logscope.connect)

media.altText: Transport selection showing J-Link RTT and Serial UART options

steps.title: View Real-Time Logs
steps.description:
Once connected, the log viewer opens automatically.

Use the **filter bar** to toggle severity levels (ERR, WRN, INF, DBG), filter by module, or search.

**HCI decoding** shows Bluetooth LE packets with expandable details when using J-Link RTT.

[Open Log Viewer](command:logscope.open)

media.altText: Log viewer showing color-coded Zephyr logs and HCI packets

steps.title: Export & Analyze
steps.description:
Export your captured logs in three formats:

- **Text** (.log) — human-readable
- **JSON Lines** (.jsonl) — structured data
- **btsnoop** (.btsnoop) — HCI packets for Wireshark

[Export Logs](command:logscope.export)

media.altText: Export format options: Text, JSON Lines, and Wireshark btsnoop

enumDescriptions: J-Link RTT via pylink (recommended — works with any J-Link device)
Serial UART (CDC ACM or USB-to-UART bridge)
logscope.transport.description: Transport mode for reading log data.

logscope.rtt.address.description: RTT control block address (hex, e.g. 0x20004050). Set to 'auto' to detect from Zephyr ELF automatically.

logscope.rtt.pollInterval.description: RTT poll interval in milliseconds (nrfutil transport only). Lower = faster updates but more SWD traffic.

logscope.nrfutil.path.description: Path to nrfutil binary. Leave as 'nrfutil' if it's on your PATH.

logscope.rtt.host.description: J-Link RTT telnet server host (jlink-telnet transport only)

logscope.rtt.port.description: J-Link RTT telnet server port (jlink-telnet transport only)

logscope.uart.baudRate.description: Serial baud rate for UART transport.

logscope.uart.lastPort.description: Last used serial port path (used for auto-connect). Set automatically.

logscope.maxEntries.description: Maximum number of log entries to keep in memory

logscope.autoConnect.description: Automatically connect to the last-used device when LogScope opens.

logscope.lastDevice.description: Last connected device (used for auto-connect). Set automatically.

logscope.logWrap.description: Wrap long log messages instead of truncating. Toggle via the wrap button in the viewer.

logscope.jlink.path.description: Path to JLinkExe. Leave empty to auto-detect.

logscope.jlink.device.description: Target device name for J-Link connection (use Cortex-M33 for nRF54L15)

logscope.jlink.autoStart.description: Automatically start J-Link when connecting. Disable to connect to an existing RTT server.

logscope.jlink.rttSearchRanges.description: RTT control block search range (base size in hex). Default covers 512KB starting at 0x20000000 which works for most ARM Cortex-M devices.
